zh.Schirmeck,Bas-Rhin,Alsace,France: 法國阿爾薩斯巴-爾希米可
zh.lifeSummary: 當Nicolas Marchal於1691年5月14日出生在法國阿爾薩斯時，他的父親Nicolas Marchal40歲，母親Marie Charton39歲。他於1722年11月18日與Maria Palmontier在法國巴-爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1730年在法國巴-爾洛索奧被列為mayor。他去世於1763年7月26日在法國巴-爾洛索奧享年72歲，埋葬在法國巴-爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Nicolas Marchal于1691年5月14日出生在法国阿爾薩斯时，他的父亲Nicolas Marchal40岁，母亲Marie Charton39岁。他于1722年11月18日与Maria Palmontier在法国巴-爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1730年在法国巴-爾洛索奧被列为mayor。他去世于1763年7月26日在法国巴-爾洛索奧享年72岁，埋葬在法国巴-爾。

nameMeanings.name: Marchal
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gilles, Jacques, Maryse, Philippe, Pierre, Veronique.

French and Walloon: status name or occupational name from Old French maresc(h)al ‘marshal’. The term is of ancient Germanic origin (from marah ‘horse, mare’ + scalc ‘servant’) and was originally applied to a man who looked after horses. In the Middle Ages it also came to be used on the one hand as an occupational name for a farrier, and on the other as a status name for an officer of state, in particular a member of a royal household with military responsibilities. Compare English Marshall and 2 below.
English: variant of   Marshall  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland, and is found mainly in Glamorgan, Wales.

variants.name: Marshall
meanings: English: usually an occupational name ‘farrier’, occasionally a status name ‘chief official of a royal household or court; a high officer of state’, from Middle English mareshal and Old French maresc(h)al. An even wider range of meanings is found in some other languages: compare for example Polish Marszałek (see Marszalek ). This name has been established in Ireland since the 13th century. It is also borne by Jews, presumably as an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.

variants.name: Marichal
meanings: French (northern), Walloon, and Spanish (Canary Islands): variant of French   Marchal  . This surname, which is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium, is established in the Canary Island since the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: Nicolas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Nicholas
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nicolao
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

historicEvents.title: French Monarchy
historicEvents.description: The French monarchy started way before the 1700s, but for the most part the major events of history took place during this time before the French Revolution started. This was known as the Bourbon Dynasty. Louis XIV the Great, Louis XV the Beloved, Louis XVI the Restorer of French Liberty, and Louis XVII all reigned during this time.

en.lifeSummary: When Nicolas Marchal was born on 14 May 1691, in Alsace, France, his father, Nicolas Marchal, was 40 and his mother, Marie Charton, was 39. He married Maria Palmontier on 18 November 1722, in Bas-Rhin, France. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. In 1730, at the age of 39, his occupation is listed as mayor in Rothau, Bas-Rhin, France. He died on 26 July 1763, in Rothau, Bas-Rhin, France, at the age of 72, and was buried in Bas-Rhin, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Nicolas Marchal est né le 14 mai 1691, à Alsace, France, son père, Nicolas Marchal, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Marie Charton, avait 39 ans. Il a épousé Maria Palmontier le 18 novembre 1722, à Bas-Rhin, France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. En 1730, à l’âge de 39 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est mayor à Rothau, Bas-Rhin, France. Il est décédé le 26 juillet 1763, à Rothau, Bas-Rhin, France, à l’âge de 72 ans, et a été enterré à Bas-Rhin, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Nicolas Marchal am 14. Mai 1691 in Elsaß, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Nicolas Marchal 40 und seine Mutter Marie Charton 39. Er hat am 18. November 1722 in Bas-Rhin, Frankreich Maria Palmontier geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. 1730, als er 39 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als mayor in Rothau, Bas-Rhin, Frankreich aufgeführt. Er starb am 26. Juli 1763 im Alter von 72 Jahren in Rothau, Bas-Rhin, Frankreich und wurde in Bas-Rhin, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Nicolas Marchal è nato il il 14 maggio 1691 nel Alsace, Francia, il suo padre, Nicolas Marchal, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Marie Charton, aveva 39 anni. Lui sposato Maria Palmontier il 18 novembre 1722, nel Bas-Rhin, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Nel 1730, all'età di 39 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come mayor nel Rothau, Bas-Rhin, Francia. Morì il 26 luglio 1763, nel Rothau, Bas-Rhin, Francia, all'età di 72 anni, e fu sepolto a Bas-Rhin, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Nicolas Marchalが1691年5月14日にフランスアルサスで生まれた時，父Nicolas Marchalは40歳，母Marie Chartonは39歳であった。1722年11月18日にフランスバス-ルヒンでMaria Palmontierと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。1730年，39歳の時，フランスバス-ルヒンロサウでの職業はmayorと記録されている。彼は1763年7月26日にフランスバス-ルヒンロサウで72歳で死去し，フランスバス-ルヒンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Nicolas Marchal(이)가 1691년 5월 14일 프랑스 알사스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Nicolas Marchal(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Marie Charton(은)는 39세였다. 그는 1722년 11월 18일 프랑스 바스-히에서 Maria Palmontier(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1730년, 39세의 나이로 그의 직업은 프랑스 바스-히 로스오에서 mayor이었다. 그는 1763년 7월 26일 프랑스 바스-히 로스오에서 72세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 바스-히에 묻혔다.

pt.14 May 1691–26 July 1763: 14 de maio de 1691–26 de julho de 1763
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Nicolas Marchal nasceu em 14 de maio de 1691, em Alsace, França, seu pai, Nicolas Marchal, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Marie Charton, tinha 39 anos. Ele casou-se com Maria Palmontier em 18 de novembro de 1722, em Bas-Rhin, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Em 1730, com 39 anos, sua profissão aparece como mayor em Rothau, Bas-Rhin, França. Ele faleceu em 26 de julho de 1763, em Rothau, Bas-Rhin, França, com 72 anos, e foi sepultado em Bas-Rhin, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nicolas Marchal родился 14 мая 1691 г. в местности алсеис, Франция, его отцу по имени Nicolas Marchal было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Marie Charton, 39. Он женился на женщине по имени Maria Palmontier 18 ноября 1722 г. в местности бас-рин, Франция. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. В 1730 году, в возрасте 39 лет,  он работал по профессии mayor в местности ротао, бас-рин, Франция. Он умер 26 июля 1763 г. в местности ротао, бас-рин, Франция в возрасте 72 лет и был похоронен в местности бас-рин, Франция.

es.14 May 1691–26 July 1763: 14 de mayo de 1691–26 de julio de 1763
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Nicolas Marchal nació el 14 de mayo de 1691, en Alsace, Francia, su papá, Nicolas Marchal, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Marie Charton, tenía 39 años. Se casó con Maria Palmontier el 18 de noviembre de 1722, en Bas-Rhin, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. En 1730, a la edad de 39 años, su ocupación registrada era mayor en Rothau, Bas-Rhin, Francia. Murió el 26 de julio de 1763, en Rothau, Bas-Rhin, Francia, a la edad de 72 años, y fue sepultado en Bas-Rhin, Francia.
